description.ko: 잊힌 위성 수신장이 FLORA-9의 평화로운 초원이 되어 낡은 신호를 영원한 봄의 프랙탈 꽃으로 피워낸다.
description.en: A forgotten satellite field becomes a peaceful FLORA-9 meadow where fractal blossoms turn obsolete signals into eternal spring.

prompt: FLORA-9 has overtaken a silent weather-satellite receiving field on a misty spring hillside, turning obsolete parabolic dishes, rusted antenna masts, cracked signal cabinets, and buried coaxial conduits into a calm digital meadow. Each dish faces the pale sky like a giant botanical calyx, its metal mesh softened by emerald #50C878 moss and lace-thin root circuits that trace the old radio patterns. From the feed horns and cable junctions bloom fractal flowers with countless nested petals, soft #FFB6C1 at their centers fading into transparent #89CFF0 along the outer rims, each petal carrying delicate veinwork like living circuitry under dew. Deep #4A0E4E plum shadow gathers inside the hollow dishes and beneath the equipment sheds, while pastel morning light glows through drifting fog. Fiber-optic vines climb the antenna lattice in slow spirals, converting abandoned transmissions into gentle pulses of spring growth. A dense field of luminous code-pollen and tiny seed-like particles floats upward around the dishes, peaceful and suspended, as if the sky itself is receiving a garden signal that will bloom forever. Intricate botanical geometry, soft pastel illumination, high particle density, serene digital nature, nature patiently reclaiming forgotten communication technology.
